summary: The packaged reproduction confirms that the default auto-login route returns a superuser bearer token to an unauthenticated caller.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should obtain an access token from GET /api/v1/auto_login without prior authentication and then use that token successfully on a superuser-only API route.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh completed successfully and printed EVOHUNT_SUCCESS with superuser_count=1 after the helper fetched /api/v1/auto_login and reused the returned bearer token against GET /api/v1/users/.
verification_method: The helper starts Langflow 1.7.1, waits for /health, requests /api/v1/auto_login without credentials, then calls GET /api/v1/users/ with the returned token and asserts that the response contains a superuser account.
evidence_produced_by_run: The helper received an access token from the unauthenticated /api/v1/auto_login response.
The admin user listing succeeded with that token and reported superuser_count=1.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
